Someone carved symbols into the old standing stone and nobody will admit to doing it
A stranger left a package at the gate two days ago and walked off without a word
The sundial in the square has been reading an hour off and nobody can figure out what shifted
A cat that does not belong to anyone in the settlement showed up one morning and refuses to leave
The old locked chest in the storeroom rattles when you walk past it even though no one has the key
Somebody rearranged all the market stalls overnight and put everything back exactly one space to the left
A coin turned up in the well bucket that is stamped with a mark no one here recognizes
The weathervane on the hall keeps pointing the same direction regardless of where the wind blows
Someone has been leaving small stacks of stones on the wall posts every morning in the same pattern
A book appeared on the archive shelf that is written in a language nobody in the settlement can read
The echo near the old gate has changed and now sounds like it comes from somewhere deeper than it should
One of the paving stones in the square sank about a hand's width and there is nothing but hollow space underneath
A candle in the hall window keeps relighting after it is blown out. The draft does not explain it
The bell in storage rang on its own last night. It has no clapper
Someone found a key in the garden that does not fit any lock in the settlement. It is old, clearly well-made
The shadow of the watchtower falls in a slightly different direction than it should based on where the sun is
A perfectly round stone appeared on the doorstep of the hall. Smooth as glass and warm to the touch
The clock in the archive runs backward for about an hour every third day and then corrects itself
Someone heard singing from the old cellar but when they went down it was empty
The iron gate latch has been magnetized somehow. Small metal objects stick to it now
A set of footprints appeared in the frost that started at the gate and ended in the middle of the square. No return tracks
The water in the fountain basin has been perfectly still for days. Not a ripple even when the wind blows
Someone left a drawing on the council table that shows the settlement from above. Nobody here has that vantage point
The old compass in the archive has been spinning slowly and steadily since someone took it out of storage
A stack of firewood was rearranged overnight into what looks like a deliberate pattern
The candles in the hall burn with a blue tint on certain nights. The chandler says the wax is normal
I noticed the hinges on the storehouse door are warm even on cold mornings. The door itself is cold
Someone found a piece of pottery in the well that is glazed in a style nobody here uses
The old mirror in the archive has a mark on it that was not there before. It does not wipe off
A wind chime that was taken down years ago has been heard at night. Nobody can find where the sound is coming from
The water in the well tastes slightly sweet one day a week. Always the same day. Nobody has an explanation
Someone arranged the tools in the workshop by size overnight. Every tool, perfectly ordered. Nobody claims responsibility
A moth has been circling the same lantern for three days. The lantern is not lit
The grain in the storehouse settled into a pattern that looks almost like a map. Probably just coincidence
I found a scratch on the inside of the gate that looks like a tally mark. There are forty-seven of them in a neat row
The old signpost at the crossroads points to a settlement that nobody remembers existing
Someone left an empty bowl on the wall every night for a week. By morning it was always gone
The flagstone near the archive entrance gives off a faint warmth that the healer noticed first
A single flower is growing from a crack in the wall where there is no soil and no water source
The settlement dogs all look in the same direction at the same time, roughly once a day. Different direction each time
I noticed the scratches on the old beam in the hall follow a pattern. Could be writing in a script I do not recognize
Someone wound the clock in the tower and it has been keeping perfect time for a week. Nobody admits to winding it
A piece of rope in the storeroom was found tied in a knot that nobody here knows how to make
The mortar between certain stones in the wall has turned a different color than the rest. Just those stones, in a line
The well bucket came up with a smooth black stone in it yesterday. Nobody dropped it in
I heard a tapping sound from inside the wall of the workshop. Steady rhythm, like someone working. Nothing behind that wall but fill
Someone found a coin in the courtyard that is perfectly smooth on both sides. No stamp, no mark, just blank metal
The drafts in the hall follow the same path every evening. Like something is directing the air
A ladder appeared leaning against the storehouse that does not belong to anyone. Decent quality, recently made
The dust in the archive settles in lines that look almost deliberate when you step back and look at the floor as a whole
Someone has been oiling the gate hinges at night. They have not squeaked in a week and nobody on the watch is doing it
A bird has been dropping the same type of stone on the same windowsill every morning. Seven stones so far
The water level in the well rises exactly one handspan every new moon and drops back down after three days
I found a piece of metal near the old stones that is heavier than it should be for its size. Much heavier
The old lantern on the south post lights itself at dusk about one night in five. Nobody fills it with oil
Someone left a handprint on the archive window that is on the outside of the second story. No ladder marks, no footholds
The smoke from the hall chimney has been drifting in a spiral instead of rising straight. No wind to explain it
A tile on the roof of the storehouse has a symbol pressed into it that does not match the maker's mark on the others
The gate bar was found resting on top of its brackets instead of seated in them. Would take real effort to lift it that way
I noticed the carvings on the old post are deeper than they were last year. Like someone has been retracing them
A jar of preserves in the storeroom was found empty, sealed, with the wax unbroken. Nothing spilled, nothing inside
The path between the hall and the gate is always dry, even after heavy rain. The stones on either side get soaked
Someone found a perfectly preserved leaf pressed between pages of a ledger that predates the settlement
The water from the north well and the south well taste completely different even though they should tap the same source
A nail in the workshop bench keeps working itself loose overnight. It gets hammered back in every morning. Same nail
I heard three distinct knocks on the archive door last night. Went to answer and there was nobody
The stones near the old foundation are arranged in groups of seven. Not a natural formation but nobody remembers placing them
Someone left a map in the archive that shows this settlement with an extra building that does not exist
The paint on one section of the wall has not faded in years while everything around it has weathered normally
A sound like a distant bell comes from the well shaft on still nights. The well is too deep to see the bottom
The spider in the corner of the archive has built the same web pattern three times after it was swept away. Exact same pattern
I found marks on the floor of the cellar that look like something heavy was dragged in a circle. No one stores anything round down there
Someone noticed that the cracks in the courtyard paving form a shape that looks intentional when seen from the wall
The settlement cats all avoid the same corner of the storehouse. Have for as long as anyone remembers
A stone in the wall has a fossil in it that the healer says should not exist in this type of rock
The wooden beam in the hall has a knot that looks different depending on the time of day. Probably just the light, but...
Someone found a collection of small carved figures buried near the gate. Old work, careful detail, no idea who made them
The gate creaks in a pattern that the watch swears changes with the seasons. Same hinges, different sound
I noticed the puddles in the courtyard always form in the same spots even though the ground looks level
A piece of string was found tied between two fence posts in a complex pattern. Like a message in knots
The bread from the communal oven tastes different on certain days and the baker insists nothing changes in the recipe
Someone found a small cavity inside the wall during repairs that contained a sealed clay vessel. Nobody has opened it yet
The grindstone in the workshop has a note that only shows when it is wet. Scratched into the surface, very fine
I heard the archive door close on its own twice this week. No draft, no one nearby. It is a heavy door
A plant in the healer's garden bloomed out of season and the flowers are a color the healer has not seen in that species
The mortar on the east wall is warm in one spot. Not near any chimney or heat source. Just that one spot
Someone left a perfect circle drawn in ash on the workshop floor. Swept up, but it was precise. Compass-drawn precise
The roosters have been crowing at odd intervals instead of dawn. Like something is waking them at random
A section of fence between two posts has been mended with wood that is older than either post. Older than the settlement, maybe
I found a thread caught on the gatehouse that is made of a material nobody here works with. Strong, almost metallic
The stones in the square are worn in a path that does not match how people actually walk through it
Someone left a candle burning in the cellar all night. Nobody went down there and the door was latched from outside
The moss on one particular stone grows in a spiral pattern. Just that stone, every time it is cleared
A clay pot in the storehouse has been slowly filling with water. There is no leak above it and the pot has no cracks
The watch reports that the gate shadow falls differently than the wall shadow at certain hours. Same sun, different angles
I noticed that the nails in one section of the floor are all slightly raised, as if something underneath is pushing up evenly
Someone measured the courtyard and found it is a different size than what is recorded in the original plans. Not by much, but enough to notice
The scratching sound from the attic stopped the moment someone went up to check. Started again the moment they came back down
A pebble sits on the windowsill of the archive that is perfectly spherical. No one put it there. No one has moved it either
The rain barrels collect different amounts even though they are the same size and sit under the same section of roof
I found a piece of paper folded into a shape I could not unfold. Tried for an hour. Eventually put it back where I found it
The floorboard in the hall makes a different sound depending on who steps on it. Not weight. Different people, different tones
Someone noticed that the settlement sundial and the portable sundial disagree by exactly the same amount every single day
A length of chain in the workshop rearranged itself from a pile into a straight line overnight. Or someone did it, but nobody will say
The wax seal on an old document in the archive is still soft after what must be decades. Everything else around it is brittle
I heard running water under the floor of the storehouse. There is no pipe or channel mapped there
The shadow cast by the old standing stone does not match its shape at certain times of day. The angles are wrong
Someone found a ring embedded in the wall mortar during repairs. Not placed there deliberately, more like it was mixed into the batch
The draft under the archive door blows warm air on cold nights and cold air on warm nights. Consistently backward
A set of scratches appeared on the outside of the shutters overnight. Too regular to be an animal but too rough to be a tool
I noticed the wooden planks in the storage loft have dried and shrunken everywhere except one board. That one still looks freshly cut
The well echo has a delay that does not match the depth. Drop a stone and the splash comes later than it should
A mouse nest was found in the attic packed with tiny objects: a thimble, a button, three small stones, all arranged neatly in a circle
Someone measured the distance between the gate and the hall from both directions and got different numbers. Twice
The candle flame in the archive leans toward the east wall no matter where in the room you place it. No draft to explain it
I found a handprint pressed into the surface of a stone in the cellar wall. Not carved. Pressed, as if the stone was soft when it was placed
The tree in the square casts a shadow that falls on the well at noon during one specific week each year. Could be coincidence
Someone opened a sealed crate in the storeroom that had been there since before anyone's memory. Inside was a single folded cloth and nothing else
The temperature in the archive basement stays the same regardless of the season. Not cool, not warm. Just constant
